skill.name: meme-factory
skill.description: Generate memegen.link meme URLs and markdown-ready meme content for social posts, code reviews, documentation, and team updates.
skill.summary: Create contextual visual and text memes with memegen.link templates and markdown examples.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives from markdown examples, URLs, and ordinary meme text. The skill is publishable with warnings because it intentionally creates third-party memegen.link image URLs and includes an unescaped markdown or HTML image helper.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Meme URL Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally builds memegen.link URLs and documentation embeds those remote image links. Meme text and optional custom background URLs may be visible to the external service when a generated image is requested, so users should avoid sensitive text or private image URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and documentation clearly use api.memegen.link and describe custom background URLs. This is expected functionality, but it creates a real privacy and third-party dependency concern.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped Markdown and HTML Image Embedding
medium_findings.description: The helper inserts url and alt_text values directly into markdown or an HTML img tag. If untrusted values are rendered in a permissive markdown environment, this could create malformed markup or link injection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation is direct and no escaping is visible, but impact depends on whether callers pass untrusted values and where the rendered output is displayed.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick findings are caused by markdown code fences, inline formatting, slash-command examples, and sample CLI usage. I did not find code that runs shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples or fenced code blocks, and the Python helper does not import subprocess, os, or similar execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography matches point to ordinary meme, markdown, or template text rather than cryptographic functions. The reconnaissance matches are documentation examples such as checks, system-themed meme text, or formatting guidance, not host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, system inventory, environment access, or file inspection behavior at these locations. The matches are contextual text in a meme guide.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Image URL Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper constructs URLs against a fixed external API and can include query parameters for style or custom image handling. This should be disclosed because generated meme content leaves the local workspace when the URL is opened or embedded.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL construction is explicit and central to the skill. It is not malicious, but it is a real network-facing pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Raw Markdown and HTML Interpolation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper returns markdown and HTML strings containing caller-provided URL and alt text. Escaping would reduce rendering-context injection risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interpolates values into output strings. Exploitability depends on downstream rendering and caller input control.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick visual humor without opening a design tool. This skill creates memegen.link meme URLs, markdown embeds, and text meme formats for fast sharing.
actual_capabilities: Generates memegen.link image URLs with selected templates and top or bottom text.
Suggests common templates for code reviews, deployments, debugging, and documentation topics.
Formats spaces and special characters for memegen-compatible URL paths.
Supports image options such as dimensions, layout, font, style, and file format.
Provides markdown image embedding examples for posts, pull requests, and team updates.
Documents text-based meme formats such as greentext, chat logs, and ASCII-style examples.
limitations: Generated image links depend on the external memegen.link service.
The helper script returns URLs and markdown, not local image files.
Custom backgrounds require public image URLs and can expose those URLs externally.
The built-in template list is limited and does not validate every live memegen template.

use_cases.title: Add Humor to Engineering Updates
use_cases.description: Create a concise meme for deployment notices, incident updates, or pull request comments.

use_cases.title: Draft Social Media Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate quick meme links with platform-friendly dimensions for informal product or community posts.

use_cases.title: Enrich Markdown Documentation
use_cases.description: Add markdown-ready image memes or text meme formats to blog posts and internal guides.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Meme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a meme about bugs everywhere using a popular template. Keep the text short and provide a markdown-ready result.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a comparison template for a team decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a Drake-style meme comparing manual testing with automated testing. Use clear top and bottom text.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Team Update Meme
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a meme for a deployment or incident update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a meme for a successful production deployment. Include an accessible alt text suggestion and a size suitable for Slack.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Markdown Meme Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request mixing image and text meme formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a short markdown section for a technical blog post that combines one memegen image link with one text-based meme format.

output_examples.input: Create a meme about all tests passing on the first try.
output_examples.output: A Success Kid meme link with concise top and bottom text, plus accessible alt text for markdown embedding.

output_examples.input: Make a code review meme about a change that might be a feature or a bug.
output_examples.output: A Futurama Fry style meme suggestion with short captions and a markdown image embed description.

output_examples.input: Add a text meme to a deployment blog post.
output_examples.output: A compact greentext-style markdown block that preserves line breaks and supports screen readers.

best_practices: Keep meme captions short so the generated image remains readable.
Use descriptive alt text when embedding generated meme images in markdown.
Avoid sensitive project names, private URLs, or confidential incidents in remote meme URLs.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private screenshot URLs into custom background parameters.
Do not use long paragraphs as meme captions because image text becomes unreadable.
Do not assume every template exists without checking the memegen template list.

faq.question: Does this skill generate local image files?
faq.answer: No. It generates memegen.link URLs and markdown-ready references to remote images.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The documented memegen.link usage does not require an API key.

faq.question: Can I use custom image backgrounds?
faq.answer: Yes, but the background image must be publicly reachable. Do not use private or sensitive image URLs.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill creates third-party image URLs, and one helper returns unescaped markdown or HTML strings.

faq.question: What should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Avoid confidential text, private project names, internal incident details, and non-public image URLs.
